description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, square, convex glass screen framed by a metallic bezel. The casing is made of wood with a rich, medium-brown grain, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. On the right side of the front panel, there are six round control knobs arranged vertically, with two larger dials below four smaller ones, likely for channel and volume adjustment. Below the screen, a wide black speaker grille spans the width of the television, bordered by a wooden frame. The sides and back are plain, featuring the same wood grain texture without any visible vents or ports. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of analog televisions from the 1960s or 1970s.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television with a large convex glass screen, metallic bezel, and a vertical row of control knobs on the front right. The design features a wide black speaker grille beneath the screen and a rich wood grain finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex screen, metallic bezel, six control knobs on the right, and a wide black speaker grille below.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain wooden panel with visible wood grain and the depth of the television.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a featureless wooden back panel with a consistent wood grain texture.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, highlighting the wooden panel and the television's boxy shape.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic bezel, silver color, rounded corners, classic vintage style
attribute_control_button_layout: Six round buttons in a vertical row on the front right panel
